name: Simulated Essay Quality Is Evaluated with Expert Scores, Distribution Checks, and Realism Ratings
statement: Wolfe and Barber evaluate LLM-simulated essays by comparing expert scores, exact and adjacent agreement, weighted kappa, score distributions, and realism ratings across prompt strategies.
population_or_context: Student essay responses for automated-scoring data augmentation.
time_horizon: arXiv preprint submitted 2026-02-06 and retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The study stages 96 real student essays and 288 simulated essays from three prompt strategies.
caveats: Human realism ratings and score preservation are quality signals for augmentation, not full scorer-validation evidence.
evaluation_method: Expert scoring, score-agreement analysis, score-distribution comparison, and realism ratings.
reported_metrics: Exact and adjacent agreement, weighted kappa, score-distribution differences, and realism ratings across prompting methods.
limitations: The quality evidence is essay-specific and prompt-strategy-specific.
The source does not validate an automated scorer solely on synthetic responses.
Model-version details are not staged in this baseline.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the paper's simulated essay sample, prompt strategies, expert scoring, agreement, distribution, and realism evaluation descriptions.
